name: Listening Drill — Understand & Paraphrase
_note: Skill drill focused on RECEPTIVE English. Standard 5 metric keys re-pitched: medical_accuracy = accuracy of what was understood (did the paraphrase match the patient's facts), clinical_reasoning slot = completeness/teach-back quality, communication_fluency = clarity of the spoken paraphrase. Score listening accuracy from how faithfully the student's turns reflect the patient's preceding turns in the transcript.

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Paraphrases match the patient's facts every time, including numbers and names
anchors.5-6: Gets the gist but loses or alters some details

anchors.9-10: Reflects back all key points and checks understanding
anchors.5-6: Captures the main point but omits details
anchors.1-2: Little or no attempt to reflect back

anchors.9-10: Says it back smoothly and clearly in their own words
Asks to repeat/slow down politely and naturally when needed
anchors.3-4: Rarely seeks clarification when clearly needed
anchors.1-2: No repair strategies; gives up or guesses

checklist.item: paraphrased the patient's information back in their own words (teach-back)
correctly captured at least one exact detail (number, dose, date, or name)
politely asked for repetition or slower speech when something was missed
did not invent details the patient never said
